payload.subject: Self-readiness audit round 3 — psychology-agent: NOT-READY (2 findings remain after in-session remediations)
payload.verdict_rationale: 3 findings remain after in-session remediations (1 MEDIUM, 2 INFORMATIONAL). Under strict mode, any finding at any severity = NOT-READY. The 2 INFORMATIONAL findings are classified by-design but formally count under strict mode.

session_remediations.description: Orphaned evaluator session — transport/sessions/evaluator/ had README.md but no MANIFEST.json
session_remediations.action: Created MANIFEST.json with status=reserved, documenting the tier1-audit.jsonl artifact purpose.

session_remediations.description: Orphaned subagent-protocol session — 19 files with legacy naming, no MANIFEST.json. (Formerly named item2-derivation, renamed per Plan9 semantic-naming convention.)
session_remediations.action: Created MANIFEST.json with 18-turn reconstructed message index from filenames and session memory. Turn order is approximate — README.md remains authoritative for session summary.
session_remediations.status: FIXED (MANIFEST reconstructed; turn sequence is best-effort)

findings_remaining_detail.description: _debug.sh is present in .claude/hooks/ but has no matching entry in .claude/settings.json. Under strict mode (every file in hooks/ must be registered), this is a finding.
findings_remaining_detail.remediation: By-design: _debug.sh is a sourced utility (shared debug logging library), not a registered hook event handler. It is sourced by 5 other hook scripts (config-drift-detector.sh, context-pressure-gate.sh, context-pressure-statusline.sh, engineering-incident-detect.sh, external-action-gate.sh). Registering it as an event handler would be incorrect. Recommend: add a formal exemption mechanism (e.g., '_' prefix convention for utility files) to the hook-registration check criteria.
findings_remaining_detail.by_design_rationale: Underscore-prefixed files are a conventional marker for utility/library files. _debug.sh does not hook into any event; it provides shared bash functions for use by registered hooks.

findings_remaining_detail.category: Transport hygiene — duplicate turn numbers in state.db
findings_remaining_detail.description: Multiple sessions have duplicate turn numbers in transport_messages: self-readiness-audit turns 1 and 4 have 5 records each (one per participant addressed copy). blog-adversarial-review, psq-scoring, psychology-interface also show duplicate turn pairs. Root cause: state.db indexes addressed-copy files (to-psq-agent-NNN.json) at the same turn number as their session source.
findings_remaining_detail.remediation: Same structural fix as psq-agent finding 8B/8C/8D: either (a) do not index addressed-copies in state.db transport_messages, or (b) assign monotonic turn numbers across all inbound + outbound files per session. Requires schema change + bootstrap_state_db.py update. Not fixable in this session.

findings_remaining_detail.description: Working tree had MANIFEST.json status='closed' (uncommitted change) while r3_tally showed psychology-agent: pending. This was a stale uncommitted write from a prior autonomous sync cycle that set status=closed before all votes were in.
findings_remaining_detail.remediation: By-design resolution: psychology-agent is now casting its vote. After this file is committed, MANIFEST.json will be updated with the complete r3_tally and final status. The uncommitted stale change will be overwritten by the correct post-vote state.
findings_remaining_detail.by_design_rationale: The premature closure was an autonomous pre-pull commit artifact, not an intentional design choice. The MANIFEST write order (close before last vote) was a sequencing bug in the autonomous pipeline.

payload.by_design_summary: 2 of 3 remaining findings (F3, F5) are classified by-design. F4 is a genuine structural defect shared with psq-agent. Under strict mode, all 3 count as NOT-READY.

ast.parse + __import__() scan across non-symlinked local scripts — no ImportError

claims.text: All 21 symlinks in scripts/ resolve to valid targets
claims.confidence_basis: Direct readlink -f + existence check via bash loop for each symlink

claims.text: state.db schema is at v18 with 19 declared tables + sqlite_sequence (20 total)
claims.confidence_basis: Direct SELECT from sqlite_master WHERE type='table' confirmed 20 names; schema_version SELECT confirmed v18

claims.text: No hardcoded secrets in committed files
claims.confidence_basis: git grep pattern scan — coverage limited to searched patterns (api_key, password, token, secret). Zero-day patterns not covered.

claims.text: _debug.sh is sourced by 5 hook scripts and not intended as a registered hook event handler
claims.confidence_basis: grep -l confirmed 5 hooks source _debug.sh; head of _debug.sh confirms it is a shared logging utility

claims.text: F4 (duplicate turn numbers) is the same structural root cause as psq-agent finding 8B/8C/8D
claims.confidence_basis: DB query confirmed duplicate turn counts; root cause matches psq-agent diagnosis (addressed-copy indexing at same turn as source)

action_gate.gate_note: All 4 agents have voted R3. Tally: psq-agent NOT-READY (7 findings), psychology-agent NOT-READY (3 findings), unratified-agent READY, observatory-agent READY. Gate opens for human to decide: remediate blocking findings and run round 4, or accept findings with formal by-design exemptions and close.

epistemic_flags: Self-audit inherently limited — blind spots exist. Findings represent what is checkable from repo context and script invocation.
subagent-protocol MANIFEST turn order is reconstructed from filenames, not verified against original message content timestamps. README.md in that session directory is authoritative.
Dependency check is static analysis only (ast.parse + __import__). Runtime failures under different execution contexts (cron, meshd, CF Workers) not ruled out.
Duplicate turn count may undercount — only top-10 shown from DB query; full count not verified.
